Article Title: Sanju Samson: Yesterday Match Result: Sanju Samson’s ton powers CSK to 103-run demolition of MI
Article Text:
MI and CSK players after the match. (Pic credit: IPL)

Watch Ashwani Kumar opens up on Mumbai Indians' win, team mindset Ashwani Kumar opens up on Mumbai Indians' win, team mindset

Samson shines in commanding total

Hosein, bowlers dismantle MI

A dominant all-round display led by Sanju Samson and Akeal Hosein powered Chennai Super Kings to a crushing 103-run win over Mumbai Indians in a high-profile IPL 2026 clash at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday.Asked to bat first, CSK piled up an imposing 207/6, riding on Samson’s unbeaten 101 off just 54 balls. The five-time champions then dismantled MI for a paltry 104 in 19 overs, with Hosein returning match-winning figures of 4/17.CSK got off to a brisk start through skipper Ruturaj Gaikwad, who made a quickfire 22 before falling early. From there, Samson took charge, blending aggression with composure even as wickets fell around him.The right-hander smashed 10 fours and six sixes, bringing up his fifth IPL century in style off the final ball of the innings. Dewald Brevis chipped in with 21, but it was Samson’s ability to anchor the innings that proved decisive.“Feels great to score a century in Wankhede versus Mumbai,” Samson said after being named Player of the Match. “After the powerplay, we kept losing wickets. I thought it's important for a settled batsman to bat through.The game tells you what to do.”MI bowlers, including Allah Ghazanfar and Ashwani Kumar, struck intermittently, but Samson’s late assault ensured CSK crossed the 200-run mark.In response, MI’s chase never got going. The hosts slumped to 11/3 inside three overs, with Quinton de Kock among the early casualties.A brief resistance came through Suryakumar Yadav (35) and Tilak Varma (37), who stitched together a 73-run partnership. However, the rising required rate and disciplined bowling kept pressure firmly on MI.Hosein broke the stand and then ran through the lower order, while Noor Ahmad struck twice in quick succession. MI captain Hardik Pandya admitted the failure with the bat proved costly. “It was the same track… we should have just batted well,” he said.The emphatic win lifted CSK to fifth place in IPL 2026 standings, significantly boosting their net run rate, while MI slipped to eighth with five losses in seven games.With the playoff race heating up, attention now shifts from Mumbai to Bengaluru, where third-placed defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru host sixth-placed Gujarat Titans at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in a crucial encounter.
Article Summary: Chennai Super Kings beat Mumbai Indians by 103 runs in IPL 2026 clash. Sanju Samson scores unbeaten 101 as CSK post imposing total of 207/6. Akeal Hosein takes match-winning figures of 4/17 for CSK.

Article Title: Big blow for Spain: Injured Lamine Yamal ruled out for season ahead of FIFA World Cup
Article Text:
Lamine Yamal

Lamine Yamal has suffered a hamstring injury and will miss the rest of the season, but his club FC Barcelona said on Thursday that he is expected to be fit in time for the upcoming FIFA World Cup."Tests have confirmed that first-team player Lamine Yamal has a hamstring injury in his left leg," Barcelona said.Spain begin their Group H campaign against Cape Verde on June 15, followed by matches against Saudi Arabia on June 21 and Uruguay six days later.Yamal picked up the injury during Barcelona’s 1-0 win over Celta Vigo in La Liga on Wednesday."This injury is going to keep me off the pitch just when I wanted to be there the most, and that hurts even more than I can put into words," Yamal wrote on social media."This isn't the end, it's just a break. I'll come back stronger, with even more determination than ever, and next season will be better."The 18-year-old, who has scored six goals in 25 matches for Spain since his debut in 2023, won and scored a penalty in that game to seal the result.After converting the spot-kick past Ionut Radu, Yamal went down and asked for treatment, staying on the ground as teammates came to celebrate.He was later substituted, with Roony Bardghji coming on in his place."He wouldn't leave the pitch without reason," said Barcelona coach Hansi Flick. "As a coach you have to manage it, you have to live with it. It's a pity that Lamine is injured now."Barcelona’s win took them nine points ahead of Real Madrid at the top of the table with six matches left, including a Clasico on May 10.Yamal is the club’s top scorer this season with 24 goals, despite missing games in September and October due to a groin issue.He was also part of Spain’s Euro 2024-winning squad and had recently spoken about his aim to play in the World Cup.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal has been ruled out for the rest of the season with a hamstring injury. The 18-year-old picked up the injury during Barcelona's 1-0 win over Celta Vigo on Wednesday. He has scored six goals in 25 matches for Spain since his debut in 2023.

Article Title: 'Moral bankruptcy of US': Tehran hits back after Donald Trump envoy suggests replacing Iran with Italy at FIFA World Cup
Article Text:
US President Donald Trump answers questions from reporters on the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the Oval Office of the White House, Monday, Nov. 17, 2025, in Washington, as FIFA president Gianni Infantino listens. (AP)

An envoy to US President Donald Trump has asked world football body FIFA to consider replacing Iran with Italy at the upcoming World Cup, according to the Financial Times.US special envoy Paolo Zampolli told the FT, as quoted by news agency AFP, that it would be a "dream" to see Italy play at the World Cup in the United States, Mexico and Canada, even though they lost in a qualification playoff last month.The Iranian embassy in Rome responded by saying the idea showed America's "moral bankruptcy" and added that Italy did not need "political privileges" to prove its football record.The FT report said the suggestion was also linked to efforts to ease tensions between Trump and Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni after differences between them over comments related to Pope Leo XIV and the Iran war."I confirm I have suggested to Trump and (FIFA President Gianni) Infantino that Italy replace Iran at the World Cup. I'm an Italian native and it would be a dream to see the Azzurri at a US-hosted tournament. With four titles, they have the pedigree to justify inclusion," Zampolli said.Italy have won the World Cup four times, but failed to qualify for a third straight edition after losing a penalty shootout to Bosnia and Herzegovina in their playoff final."Italy has earned its greatness in football on the pitch, not thanks to political privileges," the Iranian embassy said on X."The attempt to exclude Iran from the World Cup only reveals the 'moral bankruptcy' of the United States, which is afraid even of the presence of eleven young Iranians on the field of play."Iran's participation in the World Cup has been thrown into doubt by the war with the US and Israel that broke out on February 28.The Iranian football federation (FFIRI) had said in April that it was in talks with FIFA to shift its World Cup matches from the United States to Mexico.FIFA president Gianni Infantino had said last month, during Iran’s friendly match against Costa Rica in Turkey, that Iran would take part in the World Cup and would play "where they are supposed to be, according to the draw".
Article Summary: US special envoy Paolo Zampolli said it would be a "dream" to see Italy play at the World Cup in the United States, Mexico and Canada. The Iranian embassy in Rome responded by saying the idea showed America's "moral bankruptcy"

Article Title: SAFF Women's Championship: Pakistan pull out of India-hosted football tournament
Article Text:
Representative image

Watch ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20 ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20

The draw:

NEW DELHI: Pakistan’s women’s football team has pulled out of the 2026 SAFF Women’s Championship, hosted by India, the Pakistan Football Federation (PFF) said on Thursday, as reported by news agency IANS.The South Asian Football Federation (SAFF) had earlier said that all seven member teams would take part in the tournament, "making it one of the premier women’s football events in the region."With Pakistan’s withdrawal, the tournament will now be played with six teams. The event is scheduled at the Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Margao, Goa, from May 25 to June 6.This will be the second time India hosts the SAFF Women’s Championship after the 2016 edition in Siliguri, where India won their fourth title. It will also be the second SAFF tournament in Goa. The men’s SAFF Championship in 1999 was also held at the same venue, where India beat Bangladesh in the final.After Pakistan’s exit, the remaining six teams — Nepal, India (hosts), Sri Lanka, Bangladesh (defending champions), Bhutan and Maldives — have been placed into two groups following the draw held at the SAFF Secretariat in Dhaka.The withdrawal affects Pakistan women’s football, which had been looking to gain more match experience after facing administrative issues and limited international games in recent years.Pakistan had also pulled out of the men’s junior hockey World Cup held in Tamil Nadu in November last year. They did not send their senior men’s team to the Asia Cup in Rajgir as well.India are the most successful team in the SAFF Women’s Championship, winning five titles in 2010, 2012, 2014, 2016 and 2019. Bangladesh have won the last two editions in 2022 and 2024.Group A: Nepal, Sri Lanka, BhutanGroup B: India, Bangladesh, Maldives.
Article Summary: Pakistan's women's football team pulls out of 2026 SAFF Women's Championship. The tournament will now be played with six teams. The event is scheduled at the Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Margao, Goa, from May 25 to June 6.

Article Title: Fact check: Inter Kashi not paying players and staff?
Article Text:
Inter Kashi are eighth in the ISL points table. (Image: AIFF)

New Delhi:

Watch ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20 ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20

Antonio Habas. (ISL Photo)

Antonio Habas used strong words after Inter Kashi went down 0-3 to Punjab FC in the Indian Super League (ISL) on April 20. The Inter Kashi manager congratulated Punjab FC after a strong show with goals from Samir Zeljkovic, Effiong Nsungusi Jr., and Bede Osuji. But his comments toward his own team raised eyebrows."I want to say congratulations to Punjab FC, but I don’t want to talk about the match because Inter Kashi is in a very, very complicated situation with a lot of problems in the club. I don’t want to talk about the match," said Habas."We need to talk about the situation of the players and staff with the owner of the club, because it’s impossible to compete in the ISL. This is my answer today.""I am very, very sad about this situation. We have been fighting since last season 100%, and now it’s possible that the players and staff will not receive their salaries. It’s impossible," he continued.An Inter Kashi insider clarified to TimesofIndia.com that there are no financial issues within the club. Another agent informed us that they have no information on the matter.The club source suggested that the issue Habas is pointing to could well be their ongoing legal tussle with Churchill Brothers.That process has seen Inter Kashi and Churchill Brothers approach the All India Football Federation (AIFF), the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) and now the Delhi High Court.The two clubs have been locked in a legal battle over last year's I-League title. After Churchill were crowned champions, and literally handed over the trophy, the title was awarded to Inter Kashi following a disciplinary hearing. CAS had ruled in favour of Inter Kashi, and the club were promoted to the ISL.Churchill, however, attempted to gain entry as late as after the start of the ISL season, but their efforts were unsuccessful. The club also opted against playing in the second division (now called the Indian Football League).On March 31, the AIFF had accused its Executive Committee member and Churchill Brothers CEO Valanka Alemao of exerting “sustained pressure” to facilitate their inclusion in the ISL.The statement came on the day Valanka accused AIFF president Kalyan Chaubey, vice president NA Haris and deputy secretary general M Satyanarayan of intimidation and harassment during the Executive Committee meeting.Meanwhile, Inter Kashi's first season in the ISL has, expectedly, been a challenge. It took them until the third game for their first win of the season, and the loss to Punjab halted their streak of two victories.They're eighth in the ISL points table with 11 points after nine matches played, clinching three wins along the way. Their next game is against NorthEast United FC on May 2.
Article Summary: Inter Kashi lost 0-3 to Punjab FC in the Indian Super League (ISL) on April 20. Inter Kashi are eighth in the ISL points table with 11 points after nine matches played. Their next game is against NorthEast United FC on May 2. The two clubs are in a legal battle over last year's I-League title.

Article Title: Manchester City: Manchester City leapfrog Arsenal in Premier League title race; Burnley relegated
Article Text:
Manchester City's Erling Haaland, second right, scores his side's goal against Burnley. (AP Photo)

Haaland delivers, City seize control

Burnley relegated after dismal run

Race tightens, drama builds

Manchester City edged ahead in the Premier League title race after a hard-fought 1-0 win over Burnley FC at Turf Moor, a result that not only sent Pep Guardiola’s side top but also confirmed Burnley’s relegation.A first-half strike from Erling Haaland proved decisive as City moved above Arsenal FC on goals scored, with both teams locked on points and five matches remaining in a gripping title battle.City, fresh from a crucial win over Arsenal days earlier, started with intent. Within five minutes, Haaland capitalised on a precise pass from Jérémy Doku, calmly chipping the ball over goalkeeper Martin Dúbravka to hand the visitors an early lead.The defending champions dominated proceedings, repeatedly testing Dubravka, while Rayan Cherki came close with a long-range effort that rattled the woodwork. Despite controlling the game, City failed to extend their advantage, much to the frustration of manager Pep Guardiola.“The chances were there. We created a lot. We made a fantastic game,” Guardiola said, while also expressing disappointment at his side’s inability to convert dominance into a bigger margin.Burnley nearly punished that profligacy before half-time when Zian Flemming dragged a golden opportunity wide. Haaland later struck the post after the break, but City had to settle for a narrow win.The defeat confirmed Burnley’s relegation to the Championship for the third time in five seasons. Managed by Scott Parker, the Clarets have endured a torrid campaign, collecting just 20 points from 34 matches.“We’ve fallen a bit short… in certain moments we’ve lacked quality,” Parker admitted, as his side slipped 13 points from safety with only four games left.Burnley’s drop continues a pattern of promotion and relegation struggles, highlighting the gap between the Premier League and the second tier.At the top, City’s victory sets up a tense finale. Although they now lead the standings, Guardiola’s men face a tougher run-in compared to Arsenal, keeping the title race finely balanced.Elsewhere, Leeds United boosted their survival hopes with a late 2-2 draw against AFC Bournemouth, while Bournemouth’s push for European qualification took a hit.
Article Summary: Erling Haaland scores only goal of the game as Manchester City beat Burnley FC 1-0 at Turf Moor. Defeat confirms Burnley's relegation to the Championship for the third time in five seasons. Leeds United and AFC Bournemouth play out 2-2 draw at Elland Road.

Article Title: Chelsea sack coach Liam Rosenior as Blues hit historic low with five straight EPL defeats
Article Text:
Chelsea's head coach Liam Rosenior (AP Photo)

Historic slump triggers ruthless call

Financial strain, dressing room cracks

Chelsea FC have sacked head coach Liam Rosenior less than four months into his tenure, bringing a swift and dramatic end to a spell marred by historic underachievement.The decision came in the wake of a 3-0 defeat to Brighton & Hove Albion — a result Rosenior himself branded “unacceptable” — and a dire run that has seen Chelsea lose five consecutive Premier League matches without scoring. It marks the club’s worst such streak since 1912, underlining the scale of the collapse.“Chelsea Football Club has today parted company with Head Coach Liam Rosenior,” the club said in a statement, admitting performances had “fallen below the necessary standards” despite the season still being alive.Rosenior, 41, had arrived in January from RC Strasbourg Alsace following the exit of Enzo Maresca. While his appointment raised eyebrows, early promise quickly gave way to a staggering downturn.Chelsea have now lost seven of their last eight matches in all competitions, including a heavy Champions League exit to Paris Saint-Germain and a League Cup elimination at the hands of Arsenal FC.The Blues currently sit seventh in the table, seven points off the Champions League places with just four games remaining — far from expectations for a squad assembled at enormous cost.Assistant coach Calum McFarlane has been placed in interim charge, beginning with a crucial FA Cup semi-final clash against Leeds United. His immediate task will be to salvage European qualification and ease the financial burden after Chelsea reported a staggering £262.4 million pre-tax loss last season.Behind the scenes, tensions had been brewing. Questions over the earlier sacking of Maresca were publicly raised by senior players including Enzo Fernández and Marc Cucurella, while inconsistent performances further strained relations between the squad and Rosenior.The club’s ownership group, led by Todd Boehly, has now dismissed five permanent managers in under five seasons, raising serious concerns about long-term stability despite over £1 billion spent on players.Chelsea confirmed they will begin a “process of self-reflection” to appoint a long-term successor, with names like Andoni Iraola, Marco Silva and Edin Terzic already linked.
Article Summary: Chelsea have sacked head coach Liam Rosenior less than four months into his tenure. The decision came in the wake of a 3-0 defeat to Brighton & Hove Albion. Chelsea have now lost seven of their last eight matches in all competitions.

Article Title: Mohammedan Sporting: ISL: A well-earned point for Mohammedan Sporting against Chennaiyin FC
Article Text:
Mohammedan Sporting vs Chennaiyin FC match. (Pic credit: ISL)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Watch Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game

CHENNAI: After all they have been through this season, Mohammedan Sporting will head home satisfied with a 0-0 draw against Chennaiyin FC in the Indian Super League at the Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium on Tuesday. A single point did not do much to their position as relegation-threatened Md Sporting remained last in the table with two points from nine matches, while CFC are ninth with nine points.Chennaiyin started strongly but Md Sporting grew into the game with a sustained spell of attack after the 20-minute mark. It began with a series of three corners, the last of which saw Makan Chothe whip in a curling cross from the left towards an unmarked Hira Mondal. At the far post, the defender stretched to guide the ball goalward but goalkeeper Mohammed Nawaz produced a fingertip save to deny the effort.Nawaz came to Chennaiyin’s rescue on a couple of occasions and kept the score board in check. Meanwhile, despite creating chances, CFC lacked the finishing touch in the final third.In the second half, Chennaiyin attempted to build from the back but Md Sporting stood firm with a well-organised back four. Midfielder Alberto Noguera dropped deep, which limited his creative influence. CFC coach Clifford Miranda turned to his bench, bringing on fresh legs Inigo Martin and ‘local lad’ S. Prakadeswaran, yet the home side struggled to break through.
Article Summary: Mohammedan Sporting draw 0-0 with Chennaiyin FC in Indian Super League. Md Sporting remain last in the table with two points from nine matches, while CFC are ninth with nine points. CFC coach Clifford Miranda brought on fresh legs Inigo Martin and ‘local lad’ S. Prakadeswaran.

Article Title: Lamine Yamal: ‘Hope to achieve half of what Messi did’: Lamine Yamal aims to follow legend’s footsteps
Article Text:
Lamine Yamal at 2026 Laureus World Sports Awards ceremony in Madrid. (AP Photo)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

MADRID: At just 18 years of age, Lamine Yamal is already being spoken of as football’s next global superstar, though his feet remain firmly on the ground. The Rocafonda-born winger, raised just outside Barcelona, has enjoyed a meteoric rise with FC Barcelona, playing a pivotal role in a remarkable 2025 season that saw the club secure La Liga, the Spanish Super Cup, and the Copa del Rey, alongside a deep run in the UEFA Champions League Such success has inevitably drawn comparisons to Lionel Messi , the iconic figure who defined an era at Barcelona across 21 years. Yet Yamal has been quick in rejecting those parallels. For him, Messi is untouchable.Speaking after receiving his second successive honour at the Laureus World Sports Awards, this time as ‘World Young Sportsperson of the Year’, Yamal expressed deep humility.“For me, Messi is the greatest football player in history. I hope I can follow in his footsteps but I’d already be grateful to achieve even half of what he has.“He is a legend and I don’t find myself worthy compared with him. I only hope to achieve half of what he has done, he is without a doubt the best in the history of football,” he said.“When you realise an athlete isn’t just a legend of their sport but of all sports... Messi, who for me is the best player in history, and if he isn’t the greatest athlete ever, he’s definitely in that conversation with all of them. He’s more than an idol.I think everyone respects him for everything he’s done. He’s been part of the childhood of every kid when we played in the park or at school and I hope I’ll be able to follow in his footsteps,” added Yamal.Despite the accolades, Yamal is acutely aware of the responsibility that comes with fame at such a young age. “It’s hard to realise you are a role model and that you can’t make mistakes,” he admitted,“But I see it as something positive. Whenever I send a message, I try to make it meaningful and helpful.”Yamal embraced the evolving relationship between players and fans on social media. “People are entertained by what you do off the pitch too and I like this change. This has strengthened my bond with fans.”Yamal’s ambitions remain clear to fulfill his childhood dream of playing in the World Cup this summer. “I’ve always imagined playing in a World Cup, seeing my mother in the stands, representing my country. I hope we can win it,” he said.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal was named World Young Sportsperson of the Year at the Laureus World Sports Awards in Madrid. The 18-year-old winger has enjoyed a meteoric rise with FC Barcelona. Yamal has been compared to Barcelona legend Lionel Messi. The winger is aware of the responsibility that comes with fame.

Article Title: Neymar Injury: ‘Only a fit Neymar should be at the World Cup’: Cafu says it’s a race against time
Article Text:
Cafu with fans (Getty Images)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

ALSO READ:

MADRID: In a city that breathes football, Monday morning brought a feeling of nostalgia. Three legends of the game, Ruud Gullit, Luis Figo and Cafu, shared one stage inside the iconic Palacio de Cibeles at the Laureus World Sports Awards (LWSA) here.Nearly an hour ahead of schedule, the buzz in the room reflected the magnitude of the occasion with journalists from across the globe having filled every available seat. When Gullit, Figo and Cafu finally emerged, dressed casually, the trio looked as composed and fit as ever, greeting the crowd with easy smiles. The interaction ranged across topics from the upcoming World Cup to the Champions League final and the evolution of the modern game.Naturally, the conversation kicked-off to contenders for the next global crown this summer. And all three legends largely picked a similar group of favourites — France, Argentina, Brazil and Spain.Figo, widely regarded as one of the finest wingers of his generation, leaned on history and pedigree. “In terms of history, I always think of Brazil, Argentina, France and Spain,” the former Portugal captain said, while also backing his home nation, “I hope Portugal can compete and fight, but those teams have a longer track record.”Dutchman Gullit added the Netherlands to the list, saying the Dutch can’t be ignored with a strong footballing legacy.Attention soon shifted to individual stars, particularly Neymar jr and Cristiano Ronaldo — two icons facing uncertain roles ahead of the World Cup. Cafu was measured in his assessment of Neymar, placing fitness above sentiment. “If he is fit, he should be there. But only if he is fit — it’s a race against time,” he said, referring to the forward’s injury struggles. Neymar hasn’t played for the national team since suffering a knee injury in Oct 2023.Figo echoed a more assured stance on Ronaldo, backing his inclusion without hesitation. “Of course he deserves to be there. He has been a very successful player for Portugal. No doubt,” he stated.With the discussion veering towards the decline of Italy on the international stage, Gullit pointed to a loss of identity.“They forgot their DNA,” he remarked. “Italy was always built from defence. That’s how they won. Now they try to play a different football, and they lose what made them strong. They don’t have good strikers as well,” he said.The conversation also explored how football itself has transformed. From technology to player conditioning, the modern game bears little resemblance to the one these legends dominated. Gullit highlighted the impact of VAR and advancements in training, noting how players today enjoy longer careers and faster-paced matches.“The game becomes quicker every year,” he said, reflecting on improved pitches, athleticism and tactical shifts.“Where once physicality dominated, today’s game favours agility, intelligence and speed,” he added.As the session drew to a close, the focus shifted to club football and the anticipated Champions League final. Figo expressed strong belief in Paris Saint-Germain, citing their midfield strength as a decisive factor. Gullit, however, took a broader view, suggesting that the true final had already taken place between PSG and Bayern Munich in the semifinals — a clash of attacking philosophies and youthful energy.(The correspondent is in Madrid as a guest of Laureus World Sports Awards)
Article Summary: Ruud Gullit, Luis Figo and Cafu spoke at the Laureus World Sports Awards in Madrid. The three legends discussed the upcoming World Cup, Champions League final and evolution of the modern game. All three picked France, Argentina, Brazil and Spain as favourites for the tournament.

Article Title: Uneven playing field: How ISL's 'inequality' could cost clubs more than just points
Article Text:
At the time of writing, East Bengal have the best home record in the ISL this season. (Image: AIFF)

New Delhi:

Watch ISL commercial rights: AIFF get two different bids ISL commercial rights: AIFF get two different bids

Mohun Bagan Super Giant are top of the ISL standings. (AIFF)

Mohammedan Sporting, bottom of the ISL standings, have played just two home games this season. (AIFF)

The ongoing Indian Super League (ISL) season is into its ninth matchday with Mohun Bagan Super Giant top of the standings. They have Mumbai City FC, FC Goa, East Bengal FC, Bengaluru FC and Jamshedpur FC hot on their heels. At the other end, Mohammedan Sporting are in prime position to go down. But the league table only tells half the story.Mohammedan Sporting have played eight games so far and have one point to show for it - a draw against Odisha FC on April 17. Of the eight games, only two have come at home -- against FC Goa (0-2 on February 20) and Bengaluru FC (1-2 on March 7). Based on the current fixture list, by the end of the season, they would have played only three home games.While it doesn't need explaining, home advantage matters. Just ask the two other Bengal clubs. Of Mohun Bagan Super Giant's nine games and 20 points, five have been at home, earning them 12 points. East Bengal have played seven home games out of eight overall, pocketing 12 points out of 15 overall in the process.East Bengal's upcoming 'home' fixture, against Odisha FC, will be played in Goa due to elections in West Bengal. They will return to home base to face Punjab FC on May 11.In all, nine of East Bengal's 12 scheduled matches would have been played at home.The inequality isn't restricted to the ISL alone. Rajasthan United, who finished third in Stage 1 of the Indian Football League and boasted the best home record, will be denied taking that momentum into the Championship Stage. Following the All India Football Federation's (AIFF) directive on mandatory floodlights , RUFC will instead play their home matches over 500 kilometres away at Namdhari's facilities in Ludhiana.Returning to the ISL, Odisha FC have also had a change in its home venue. Their upcoming 'home' matches against Bengaluru FC (May 4) and Punjab FC (May 16) will be played in Goa.Varanasi-based Inter Kashi have played three 'home' games in Kolkata while their own stadium is being built; this number will increase to four by the end. Mohali-based Punjab FC have played its home fixtures in New Delhi.Amid these inequalities, the ramifications of a poor - or strong - season apply. The team that doesn't do well in the ISL will be relegated to the IFL and the team that does well in the IFL will be promoted. The relegated teams will be impacted by a drop in revenue, business opportunities, broadcast coverage, among others. Meanwhile the team that wins the title will earn higher prize money, play continental football, be able to interest the bigger talents etc.Yet, force majeure remains a hushed topic within AIFF corridors . The current AIFF Constitution, ratified by the Supreme Court, mandates promotion and relegation in India's top division. It also puts the ISL in compliance with the Asian Football Confederation (AFC) Statutes.In 2019, AIFF, clubs, and then commercial partner Football Sports Development Limited (FSDL) accepted the AFC's proposal to have relegation starting from the 2024-25 season. That promise will be kept from this season onwards.In January, all 14 ISL clubs wrote to the Sports Ministry and asked for relegation to be scrapped for the season and three to five seasons thereafter. They asked that the 2025-26 season be considered a "virtual force majeure." There has been no headway on that matter since.The FIFA and Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) rulings on similar cases in the past might not help the ISL clubs get away from the liability of paying salaries. But the fact that this has not been a equal season for all teams cannot be ignored.Yet, the fact that promotion and relegation will take place across India's top two divisions is a grave injustice after what the AIFF chief Kalyan Chaubey himself described as a "crisis."
Article Summary: Indian Super League (ISL) season is into its ninth matchday. Mohun Bagan Super Giant are top of the ISL standings. At the other end, Mohammedan Sporting are in prime position to go down. East Bengal have the best home record in ISL this season.

Article Title: Caleb Downs: Malik Nabers left stunned as the Dallas Cowboys hijack New York Giants plans and trade up to draft Caleb Downs
Article Text:
Malik Nabers was left stunned as the Dallas Cowboys hijacked the New York Giants' plans and traded up to draft Caleb Downs (Image via Getty)

Dallas Cowboys move ahead of New York Giants to grab Caleb Downs and spark Malik Nabers reaction

The moment the draft board flipped, the tone changed. The Dallas Cowboys made their move on April 24, 2026. They jumped one spot to No. 11 and took Caleb Downs. That single move hit the New York Giants hard. It also sparked a viral reaction from Malik Nabers, who could not hide his feelings at that moment.The clip spread fast because it felt real. Nabers did not hold back. He reacted like a player who knew what was coming. The Giants had a strong interest in Downs. Then Dallas stepped in and changed the plan in seconds.The Dallas Cowboys entered the first round with the No. 12 pick. Then they saw their window. They traded up to No. 11 and secured Downs, one of the most complete defensive backs in the class. The former Ohio State star arrived with elite range, sharp instincts, and a strong reputation as a playmaker.The Giants had already made moves earlier that night. They selected linebacker Arvell Reese at No. 5. They also picked Francis Mauigoa at No. 10 after moving on from Dexter Lawrence. Even then, Downs stayed on their radar. Dallas read that situation and acted fast.Nabers watched it unfold live alongside Micah Parsons during a draft stream on April 24, 2026. That is when the viral moment happened. Speaking on the live stream, Nabers said, “I’d rather get him than play against him… I gotta play against this thing again. He’s good, but he’s very good. We all got him No. 1.” His tone said everything. This was respect mixed with concern.That reaction carries weight because Nabers knows the matchup. As a top receiver, he studies defensive backs closely. Downs is not just another rookie. He brings physicality and awareness that can disrupt passing games. That is exactly what Dallas needed.His college resume backs that belief. Downs started all 44 games across his time at Alabama and Ohio State. He finished with 256 tackles, 16 tackles for loss, six interceptions, and 16 pass breakups. He also won the Jim Thorpe Award in 2025 as the nation’s top defensive back. More importantly, he did not allow a single touchdown as a primary defender during his Ohio State run. That tells you why Dallas pushed the button.Now the balance in the division shifts slightly. The Cowboys add a major piece to their secondary. The Giants miss out on a player they rated highly. Nabers now faces the reality he spoke about. He will line up across from Downs, not alongside him.The draft always brings surprises. This one sent a clear message. Dallas stayed aggressive. New York got beaten to a key target. And Nabers voiced what many around the league already felt.
Article Summary: Dallas Cowboys traded up to No. 11 and took Ohio State defensive back Caleb Downs. The move hit the New York Giants hard and sparked a viral reaction from Malik Nabers, who could not hide his feelings at that moment. Nabers said, “I’d rather get him than play against him’

Article Title: Mike Vrabel: Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini controversy takes a new turn as former NFL reporter friend hints ‘more is coming’ after fresh TMZ photos
Article Text:
Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini controversy takes a new turn as a former NFL reporter friend hints ‘more is coming’ after fresh TMZ photos (Getty Images)

Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini's controversy deepens as fresh photos emerge and Dan Le Batard hints at more ahead



The spotlight on Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini is not fading. Instead, it is getting louder. On top of that, fresh photos published by TMZ have added a new layer to an already tense situation. This time, the images are placed both at a Mississippi casino. The timing and setting have pushed the story back into the news cycle.At the same time, a new voice has raised the stakes. Dan Le Batard, who shares a personal connection with Russini, believes this is not over yet. His comments have shifted the focus from what is known to what might still come out. That warning has now become the biggest talking point around the Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy.The latest images show Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini at the Beau Rivage Casino in Mississippi. The sighting came shortly after Vrabel’s exit from the Tennessee Titans. According to TMZ’s report, a witness said the two appeared comfortable while spending time at gaming tables. There was no public display of affection, but the body language drew attention after recent reports.This comes days after photos from Sedona, Arizona, first reported by Page Six, surfaced.That initial leak sparked questions about professional boundaries. Both Vrabel and Russini denied any wrongdoing and said the images lacked context. Still, the conversation has not slowed down.At the same time, Dan Le Batard shared another update. On his show, “The Dan Le Batard Show with Stugotz,” Dan Le Batard addressed the situation. He spoke carefully, but made his stance clear. “I’ve got somebody who I care about, who must be feeling pretty terrible right now. And I don’t want to make any of this worse…This is a nightmare for the parties involved. Everybody involved. This is a real public nightmare. There are kids involved. And everybody clucking about this at work and everywhere else”.He further added, “I understand how it is that anyone is going to criticize crisis management because it’s weird in the middle of the night to throw us counseling. But let’s check the timeline here for a second because it does feel like they’re warning us that more is coming.”That statement has now become central to the story. It hints that more details, or possibly more visuals, could surface soon. Meanwhile, ESPN reported that New England Patriots coach Mike Vrabel stepped away from Day 3 of the NFL Draft to seek counseling. He also addressed reporters earlier and promised accountability moving forward.The NFL has not opened a formal investigation. Still, the Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini situation continues to raise questions about optics and trust. With Dan Le Batard suggesting more developments ahead, this story looks far from finished.
Article Summary: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini's controversy deepens as fresh photos emerge and Dan Le Batard hints at more ahead. New England Patriots coach Mike V rabel stepped away from Day 3 of the NFL Draft to seek counseling. The NFL has not opened a formal investigation.

Article Title: Rams draft Ty Simpson from Alabama, What does it mean for Matthew Stafford’s future in Los Angeles
Article Text:
Alabama quarterback Ty Simpson poses with NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell (AP Photos)

Can Ty Simpson realistically become Matthew Stafford ’s successor in Los Angeles?



The Los Angeles Rams didn’t wait around to address their future at quarterback. With the 13th pick in the 2026 NFL Draft, they selected Alabama’s Ty Simpson, a move that quickly shifted the conversation from immediate contention to long-term planning. Simpson entered the night with a wide range of projections, rarely linked to the top half of the first round. Yet the Rams saw enough to act early. Now, the 23-year-old walks into a locker room led by the league’s reigning MVP, with time on his side and expectations quietly building.For now, Ty Simpson’s role is clearly defined. He slots in behind Matthew Stafford, who is coming off arguably the finest season of his career. At 38, Stafford remains firmly in control of the offense, but questions about how long he wants to keep playing have lingered. The Rams didn’t ignore that reality. They leaned into it.Simpson offers traits that fit what Sean McVay wants to run. His accuracy stands out, especially in tight windows. He moves well within the pocket and rarely looks rushed, even when protection breaks down. Still, there’s a gap between potential and readiness.Simpson started just one full season in college, and that lack of experience showed late in 2025 when his draft stock dipped.That context matters. The Rams are not asking him to contribute right away. Instead, this is a developmental year. Simpson will learn the system, adjust to NFL speed, and work behind a quarterback who understands every nuance of the position. It’s a patient approach, and one the franchise has used before with success.Behind Stafford, the depth chart is thin but open. Stetson Bennett IV remains in the mix, though Simpson is expected to take control of the backup role sooner rather than later. With Jimmy Garoppolo unsigned and weighing retirement, the opportunity is there.There’s also clarity in how this decision was handled. According to FOX’s Jay Glazer, the Rams informed Stafford of their plan before making the pick, emphasizing that this was about securing the future. That message matters in a veteran locker room.Reactions outside the building were mixed. “The Rams are getting ready for life without Matthew Stafford,” one person said. “They were so close last year, and opt for the future rather than the now. Stafford can’t be a happy camper right now.” Another added, “Wow did not think Ty Simpson would go this early.”Internally, though, this wasn’t a surprise. “When Ty Simpson was trying to decide whether or not he should come out of Alabama and enter the draft, his father consulted with, among other people, Les Snead, the GM of the Rams, who told him Ty was, in fact, a first-rounder,” NFL Network’s Ian Rapoport said, via On3. “Could the Rams, maybe in a trade-back situation, be looking at Simpson? We shall see.”They didn’t trade back. They didn’t hesitate. Now the timeline begins, even if the spotlight doesn’t arrive right away.
Article Summary: The Rams selected Alabama quarterback Ty Simpson with the 13th pick in the 2026 NFL Draft. He slots in behind Matthew Stafford, who is coming off arguably the finest season of his career. Simpson started just one full season in college, and that lack of experience showed late in 2025.

Taylor Swift’s first meeting with Patrick Mahomes resurfaces amid rising stress over Travis Kelce's wedding plans (Getty Image)

Article Title: ESPN analyst Mina Kimes turns heads at NFL Draft with stunning black lace look that leaves fans buzzing
Article Text:
Mina Kimes NFL Draft 2026 (Instagram)

Did Mina Kimes win draft night with her look?



Mina Kimes arrived at the 2026 NFL Draft in Pittsburgh on Thursday and gave fans plenty to talk about before a single pick was made. The ESPN analyst wore a black lace Nadine Merabi dress that flooded her social media with compliments within hours. Meanwhile, the draft itself had its own fashion moments, from Notre Dame running back Jeremiyah Love's diamond chain and comic book jacket to Ohio State receiver Carnell Tate's touching tribute to his late mother and sister.Mina Kimes has been with ESPN since 2014, starting as a senior writer for ESPN The Magazine before becoming one of the network's most recognized NFL analysts. She earned a National Headliner Award for her magazine work and earned a Sports Emmy nomination for Outstanding Sports Personality. These days, she is a fixture on NFL Live every weekday during the season.On Thursday afternoon, ESPN deployed Kimes and the rest of the NFL Live team to anchor a live show directly from Pittsburgh. And while the broadcast itself drew plenty of attention, it was her off-camera look that dominated the conversation.The 40-year-old Korean-American analyst posted a photo on Instagram before the show, adding a candid caption: "Extremely rare ponytail moment for yours truly here in Pittsburgh."The reaction was immediate."Nice dress," one fan wrote. "You do an incredible job, have fun and good luck tonight." Another said, "That dress is amazing. Wow." A third commented simply, "You look great Mina."The dress, designed by London-based label Nadine Merabi, is known for its intricate lacework and bold silhouette. It was a deliberate statement, and it landed exactly as intended.Draft nights tend to produce a parade of fashion, with prospects leaning hard into the moment. Love's enormous diamond chain and illustrated jacket made waves on the red carpet. Tate, meanwhile, wore a message dedicated to his late mother and sister, turning his draft-night look into something deeply personal.But in a sea of bold choices, Kimes stood out for a different reason. She wasn't there as a prospect waiting for her name to be called. She was there to anchor the night, and she did it with authority, both on screen and off.It is not the first time she has made an impression at a major sports event. Her appearance at the ESPYs previously earned similar attention, but Thursday felt different. The draft gives football its biggest offseason stage, and Kimes used it fully.
Article Summary: Mina Kimes wore a black lace Nadine Merabi dress at the 2026 NFL Draft. Her off- camera look dominated the conversation. The draft itself had its own fashion moments, from Notre Dame running back Jeremiyah Love's diamond chain to Ohio State receiver Carnell Tate's tribute to his late mother and sister.

Article Title: Dianna Russini And Mike Vrabel: Roger Goodell distances the NFL from Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini scandal as old photos ignite fresh controversy
Article Text:
Roger Goodell distances NFL from Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini scandal as old photos ignite fresh controversy(Getty Images)

NFL Rodgers Goodell believes Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini matter is personal as resurfaced photos stir fresh debate



The NFL is stepping back from the noise. On April 23, 2026, Roger Goodell made it clear the league will not step into the growing controversy involving New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and NFL reporter Dianna Russini. The issue gained fresh attention after older photos surfaced online, adding another layer to an already sensitive situation.The images, first published by Page Six earlier in April, show Vrabel and Russini together at a New York bar in March 2020. As those visuals made rounds again during draft week, questions picked up. Still, the league office chose to draw a line early and keep its distance.Speaking to ESPN’s Mike Greenberg just minutes before Round 1 of the 2026 NFL Draft in Pittsburgh on April 23, Roger Goodell addressed the situation directly. He did not leave room for speculation. “This is not a personal conduct policy issue as we know of today,” Goodell said on ESPN ahead of the draft. “It’s a personal matter, and we’ll leave it at that.”That direct stance truly matters. It signals the NFL sees no league violation at this stage. Instead, the focus shifts to the New England Patriots. The team backed Mike Vrabel publicly on April 23, stating he remains committed to improving himself for his family and the organization.Meanwhile, the resurfaced 2020 photos continue to drive the conversation. Reports from Page Six describe Vrabel and Dianna Russini appearing close during that outing, with eyewitness accounts pointing to affectionate behavior. At the time, both were in separate relationships, which has only intensified the scrutiny now.Vrabel responded a day earlier, on April 22, while speaking to ESPN. He confirmed he will step away from duties for Day 3 of the draft. “My family needs me this weekend, and that’s where I’ll be,” he said before the event. That decision adds a personal dimension to the story, beyond football.On the other hand, Dianna Russini has remained silent since announcing her resignation from The Athletic following an internal review into the matter. Her absence has kept the focus on new developments rather than responses.For now, the league’s position holds firm. Roger Goodell has drawn a clear boundary. The NFL will not intervene. The spotlight now remains on Mike Vrabel, Dianna Russini, and how the New England Patriots manage the fallout internally.
Article Summary: NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell made it clear the league will not step into the growing controversy involving Mike Vrabel and NFL reporter Dianna Russini. The issue gained fresh attention after older photos surfaced online, adding another layer to an already sensitive situation. The images, first published by Page Six earlier in April, show the pair together at a New York bar in March 2020.

Article Title: 2026 NFL Draft: Kansas City Chiefs trade up from no. 9 pick to select LSU cornerback Mansoor Delane at no. 6
Article Text:
Mansoor Delane drafted to Chiefs (AP Photos)

Why did the Chiefs trade up for Mansoor Delane?



The Kansas City Chiefs didn’t wait around on draft night. Needing help in the secondary, they moved up to No. 6 to select cornerback Mansoor Delane, widely viewed as the top player at his position in the 2026 NFL Draft. The deal with the Cleveland Browns cost Kansas City the ninth pick, plus third- and fifth-round selections, but it addressed a clear gap after key departures. Delane now arrives with both production and expectation, stepping into a defense that demands immediate impact.The decision becomes easier to understand when looking at what the Chiefs lost. Trent McDuffie and Jaylen Watson are no longer in the building, both now with the Los Angeles Rams. That left a noticeable hole in a system built heavily on press-man coverage. Delane fits that mold naturally.At 6th overall, Kansas City wasn’t just drafting for upside. They were betting on a player who has already proven himself at multiple levels. Mansoor Delane built his early reputation at Virginia Tech Hokies, where he earned Freshman All-America honors and later All-ACC recognition. But it was his final season at LSU Tigers football that pushed him into elite territory.He didn’t just adjust to SEC competition. He controlled it. Quarterbacks completed only 37.1 percent of passes thrown his way, a number that speaks as much to his instincts as it does his technique. His opening statement came early, a second-half interception against Clemson that set the tone for the year.Across 44 college games, Mansoor Delane’s production stayed consistent. He totaled 191 tackles, eight interceptions, and more than 30 pass breakups. Numbers like that tend to travel well to the next level, especially when paired with verified speed. His 4.38-second 40-yard dash erased any lingering doubts about whether he could keep up with NFL receivers.What stands out more is how he plays. Smooth in transition, patient at the line, and rarely out of position, Delane doesn’t rely on recovery speed. He prevents separation before it starts. That trait alone makes him valuable in Kansas City’s scheme.“They made the best move in the draft and I’m excited,” Delane said.That confidence is shared inside the organization. Moving up cost assets, but it brought clarity to a position that needed it. For a team still chasing championships, that kind of certainty often matters more than depth.
Article Summary: Mansoor Delane was widely viewed as the top player at his position in the 2026 NFL Draft. The Kansas City Chiefs traded up to No. 6 to select Delane. The deal with the Cleveland Browns cost Kansas City the ninth pick, plus third- and fifth-round selections.

Article Title: Who is Jeremiyah Love dating? What we know about the personal life of No. 3 overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft
Article Text:
Jeremiyah Love dating life (Getty Images)

Who is Jeremiyah Love dating? What we know



Jeremiyah Love is now a Arizona Cardinals player. The rising running back is the no. 3 overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft. By selecting Love, they secured one of the class’s most complete offensive weapons. The former Notre Dame Fighting Irish standout arrives with production, versatility, and a growing spotlight that now extends beyond football. As his professional chapter begins, curiosity around his life off the field has followed closely behind.Jeremiyah Love’s rise has been steady rather than sudden. He entered college football in 2023 and quietly built his résumé, appearing in all 13 games as a freshman and laying the groundwork with 385 rushing yards. What followed was a sharp climb. Over three seasons, he developed into a central figure in Notre Dame’s offense, finishing with 2,882 rushing yards and 36 touchdowns. Add 63 receptions for 594 yards, and the profile becomes clear. He is not limited to early downs or predictable situations.Evaluators took notice. Mel Kiper Jr. ranked him among the top prospects in the class and even drew comparisons to Reggie Bush. That kind of praise tends to travel fast, and in Love’s case, it has brought a wider audience along with it.Part of that attention has shifted to his personal life. Love has not publicly addressed his relationship status, but social media activity points to a connection with a user known as “uniquealiece.” Posts and interactions between the two have been noticed, including her presence at the draft in Pittsburgh and a TikTok video from 2025 that read, "What a blessing it is to watch your partner accomplish their dreams, I’m so proud of you baby ᥫ᭡. @Jlove". The visibility has sparked conversation, though neither has confirmed details outright.There is also a more reflective side to Love that surfaced months before draft night. Speaking to the Daily Citizen in November 2025 about teammate Eli Raridon, he said, “I love Eli. You know, he’s married and sets the standard outside of football. I want to get married in a couple of years or so," Love said. The comment stood out for its honesty, offering a glimpse into priorities that extend beyond the game.For the Cardinals, the focus remains on what Love brings between the lines. Still, it is clear his story resonates on more than one level. There is the player, polished and ready for the NFL. And then there is the person, navigating ambition, relationships, and expectations in real time.
Article Summary: Jeremiyah Love is the no. 3 overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft. The former Notre Dame Fighting Irish standout arrives with production, versatility, and a growing spotlight. Love has not publicly addressed his relationship status, but social media activity points to a connection with a user known as “uniquealiece”

Article Title: Who are Fernando Mendoza’s parents? What is his ethnicity, family background
Article Text:
All About Fernando Mendoza’s Parents (Getty Images)

Who are Fernando Mendoza’s parents ?

Elsa and Fernando Mendoza Sr. (ESPN College Football/Youtube)

How did family, culture and adversity shape Fernando Mendoza’s rise?

Fernando Mendoza has made history and did something Indiana has never seen. The Heisman Trophy winner, fresh off leading the Hoosiers to their first national title, is the No. 1 overall in the 2026 NFL Draft. Now he is a raiders player. It marks a historic first for the program. Mendoza has been watching the moment from Miami, surrounded by family. At the center of that circle is his mother, whose influence has shaped both his career and his outlook long before the spotlight found him.Fernando Mendoza, 22, was born to Fernando Mendoza Sr. and Elsa Mendoza, both of whom built their lives in Florida after Cuban-rooted upbringings. His father took a different path from football, carving out a career in medicine. After studying biology and rowing at Brown, Mendoza Sr. went on to earn his medical degree from the University of Florida. He now serves in pediatric emergency medicine at Nicklaus Children’s Hospital in Miami, balancing long hours with a steady presence in his son’s life.Elsa Mendoza brings a different kind of athletic history. A former college tennis player at the University of Miami, she passed down both discipline and competitiveness.But her role in Fernando’s story runs deeper than sports. In 2007, she was diagnosed with multiple sclerosis when Fernando was just four. Over time, the disease left her in a wheelchair, quietly reshaping the family’s daily reality.Mendoza rarely speaks about his journey without mentioning her. After winning the Heisman, he struggled to hold back emotion as he said: “Mommy, this trophy is as much yours as it is mine. You’re my life, you’re my why, you’re my biggest supporter. You taught me toughness doesn’t need to be loud – it’s quiet and strong, it’s believing in yourself when the world doesn’t give you much reason to. You and I are rewriting what people think is possible. ” The words landed with weight because they were lived, not rehearsed.Athletic ability runs through the Fernando Mendoza household. His father once won gold at the 1987 Junior World Championships in rowing, while Elsa competed at a high level in tennis. That competitive edge filtered down naturally. But the family’s defining moments came off the field.The Mendozas spent part of their early years in Boston, where Elsa became pregnant with Fernando at 25. The family later grew with the addition of his brothers, Alberto and Max. Football became a shared language, especially between Fernando and Alberto, both of whom led their high school to state titles as quarterbacks.For years, Elsa kept her diagnosis private from her children. It was not until 2020, when her condition worsened, that she opened up about it. By then, Fernando had already absorbed the lessons she lived daily. Quiet resilience. Consistency. Perspective.Those traits carried into his leadership at Indiana. Fluent in Spanish and proud of his Cuban heritage, Mendoza built connections across the locker room during a perfect 16-0 season. Teammates responded not just to his talent, but to his presence. There is a calm about him that mirrors his upbringing.The story around Fernando Mendoza is not just about breaking records. It is about the people who built the foundation. And in Miami, after his name was called, that story now feel complete, even if his journey is only just beginning.
Article Summary: Fernando Mendoza, 22, is the No. 1 overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft. He is fresh off leading the Hoosiers to their first national title. His mother, Elsa, was diagnosed with multiple sclerosis when he was just four.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumor: Tennessee Titans linked to blockbuster move for $15M Atlanta Falcons star after a disastrous run
Article Text:
Tennessee Titans linked to blockbuster move for $15M Atlanta Falcons star after a disastrous run.(Image via X)

Tennessee Titans tipped to make a move for the Atlanta Falcons ’ star tight end , Kyle Pitts



The Tennessee Titans experienced a disastrous run on the field in the last few seasons as they recorded just 3 wins and 14 losses in both 2025 and 2024.It seems the team failed to show any improvement in their performance even as the Tennessee Titans focused on a roster change.A few hours ago, Bleacher Report’s Alex Kay linked the Tennessee Titans to the Atlanta Falcons’ star player, Kyle Pitts.Bleacher Report’s Alex Kay noted, “The Tennessee Titans could look to capitalize on a rejuvenated Pitts being available in a trade. While fourth-round rookie Gunnar Helm showed some promise last year, Pitts would immediately slot in as the team's TE1 and become a focal point of the offense.”Bleacher Report’s Alex Kay added, “If the Titans want to build around rising star Cam Ward, it would behove the organization to put a strong offer for Pitts on the table during the 2026 draft.”Bleacher Report’s Alex Kay continued, “The tight end would be an ideal red area target and safety valve in offensive coordinator Brian Daboll's system, while still being young enough at 25 to continue improving alongside Ward if he receives a contract extension from the Titans.”However, the Tennessee Titans have not yet commented on the trade talks involving Kyle Pitts.Though the Atlanta Falcons placed the franchise tag on Kyle Pitts in February, it seems the team is still open to trade him.As per Atlanta Journal-Constitution, last week the Atlanta Falcons’ general manager Ian Cunningham commented on Kyle Pitts as he said, "I would say, for all the players, we are going to listen…”He added, "I wouldn't just say Kyle. All players, we are going to listen. That's our job. That doesn't mean that's going to happen."The Atlanta Falcons’ star tight end, Kyle Pitts, has been a force to be reckoned with on the field.Last season, even as he dealt with a brutal knee injury, Kyle Pitts started all 17 games as he finished the season with 88 catches for 928 yards and a career high five touchdowns.This also earned Kyle Pitts his first AP All-Pro selection as he impressed everyone with his dominating performance on the field.For now, all eyes are on what the Atlanta Falcons decide to do about Kyle Pitts as fans wait for him to bounce back on the field.
Article Summary: The Tennessee Titans experienced a disastrous run on the field in the last few seasons as they recorded just 3 wins and 14 losses in both 2025 and 2024. A few hours ago, Bleacher Report’s Alex Kay linked the Tennessee Titans to the Atlanta Falcons’ star player, Kyle Pitts.

Article Title: Luka Doncic injury update: What’s the status of Lakers star’s return timeline ahead of Game 3?
Article Text:
Luka Doncic. Image via: Adam Pantozzi/ NBAE via Getty Images

Latest Luka Doncic injury update



Luka Doncic was seen doing court drills



The Luka Doncic injury update remains a mix of quiet progress and firm caution as the Los Angeles Lakers prepare for Game 3. A brief glimpse of the star guard back on the practice floor sparked hope, but inside the team and around the league, expectations are still being managed carefully.With a 2-0 lead over the Houston Rockets, the Lakers have breathing room. That cushion has allowed the coaching staff to resist any temptation to rush their franchise player back. For now, the focus stays on patience, even as small signs of progress begin to surface.Luka Doncic has been sidelined since April 2 with a Grade 2 hamstring strain, an injury that rarely allows shortcuts. The initial outlook placed him out indefinitely, and that stance has not changed despite recent developments. Medical experts have been quick to temper expectations after footage showed him moving through light drills.“Glad to see Luka Dončić back on the court,” physical therapist Dr. Evan Jeffries wrote. “However, this does not look like a player that would be back in a week from the hamstring strain.”That assessment aligns with the typical recovery window of four to six weeks, sometimes longer when there is a history of similar injuries.Former NBA champion Danny Green echoed the same concern. “When I heard Grade 2, I said he’s out. He’s not coming back. That’s usually four to six weeks. But if you’ve already injured it before, you can add another two weeks on top of that.”Green also pointed to the fragile nature of the injury. “Hamstrings are very sensitive,” he said. “If you pop that thing, you’re out for a very long time.”The video that fueled recent optimism showed Doncic taking part in controlled shooting drills after practice. Working alongside assistant coach Greg St. Jean, he moved carefully through light passing and set shots, avoiding any explosive movement.It was a step forward, but only an early one. These sessions usually come before more demanding phases like sprinting, cutting, and full-contact play. Until those boxes are checked, a return to game action remains distant.The Lakers understand that reality. With LeBron James leading the charge and the team holding a strong position in the series, there is little urgency to take risks. Even a late return in the first round would depend on how quickly Doncic progresses through each stage of rehab.For now, he continues to work behind the scenes, including specialized treatment overseas aimed at speeding up recovery. Still, as the early footage suggests, progress is steady rather than sudden.
Article Summary: Luka Doncic has been sidelined since April 2 with a Grade 2 hamstring strain. A brief glimpse of the star guard back on the practice floor sparked hope. Medical experts have been quick to temper expectations after footage showed him moving through light drills.

Article Title: Caitlin Clark gets hilariously trolled by Indiana Fever best mate Sophie Cunningham ahead of WNBA return
Article Text:
Caitlin Clark and Sophie Cunningham. Image via: Getty Images

Sophie Cunningham mocks Caitlin Clark over attention span



Caitlin Clark reveals cautious approach to upcoming season



Caitlin Clark gets hilariously trolled by Indiana Fever best mate Sophie Cunningham, and the moment says plenty about the mood inside the locker room. After a season shaped by injuries and long stretches on the sidelines, Clark is back around her teammates with a lighter energy. The jokes are flying again, and so is the chemistry.For the Indiana Fever, Clark may have missed large parts of the 2025 campaign, but she stayed close to the team, often cheering from the bench. That presence helped build a strong bond, especially with Cunningham, whose personality has become a perfect counterbalance to Clark’s intensity.The latest example came during a lighthearted “Pass the Phone” segment, where Sophie Cunningham did not hold back. With a grin, she delivered a playful jab that quickly made the rounds online.“I’m passing the phone to someone who needs a tan and has the attention span of a fish,” Cunningham said.The camera cut to Caitlin Clark, who took it in stride. These two have built a rhythm around teasing each other, a dynamic that took shape during a season where both dealt with setbacks. Cunningham herself was sidelined late in the year with a torn MCL, and the shared experience only strengthened their connection.Clark has not responded publicly to the latest joke, but she has never shied away from the back and forth. Earlier, Cunningham had even joked that Clark would be the last person she would trust with her social media accounts. It is all part of a growing friendship that has become visible both on and off the court.Behind the humor, Clark’s approach to the upcoming season reflects a more measured mindset. After dealing with a groin injury and a bone bruise that limited her to just 13 games, she is adjusting how she prepares and plays."I'm the best transition player in the league," Clark said. "That's where I thrive. Everyone knows that's my game. So, getting the ball off a rebound in transition, that's probably when I'm going to be a primary ball handler more than anything, so, I think there's a lot of different things that can give us a lot of different looks and just make us harder to guard."That confidence remains intact, but so does a new level of awareness. Clark admitted it has not been easy to scale back."I'm the person that doesn't want to sit out a single rep, I want to be in there every single time," she said. "I just love competing and I love playing, and none of that has changed, but just being a little smarter with my body and understanding what it takes."Training camp has forced her to rethink old habits. "These days are really long; you're on the court for two and a half, three hours, putting in a lot of stuff, so taking care of my body at this point in the season is probably the most important thing."That perspective came from an unfamiliar place. "I've never really sat on the sidelines before, so that's a really humbling experience and it gives me a good perspective on how to be a great teammate and a great leader," she concluded.
Article Summary: Sophie Cunningham mocks Caitlin Clark over attention span on "Pass the Phone" Cunningham and Clark have built a friendship around teasing each other. Cunningham was sidelined late in the year with a torn MCL, and the shared experience only strengthened their connection.

Article Title: Indiana Fever’s Sophie Cunningham shares a personal update ahead of her WNBA return
Article Text:
Sophie Cunningham. Image via: Erica Denhoff/ Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

Sophie Cunningham gets baptized



Sophie Cunningham. Image via: Andrew J. Clark/ISI Photos/ISI Photos via Getty Images

Sophie Cunningham on injury recovery



The Indiana Fever’s Sophie Cunningham update arrives with clarity and conviction, just days before her expected return to the court. Cunningham has chosen to speak openly about where she stands, both as a player and as a person. It is not just about basketball right now.As the Indiana Fever prepare for a new season, Cunningham’s voice has carried across media day and training camp. She addressed contract chatter, shared a personal milestone, and quietly reminded everyone why she remains one of the team’s most grounded figures heading into a crucial year.Sophie Cunningham marked a significant personal moment shortly after signing her one-year deal, sharing a video of her adult baptism. The decision, she explained, came from a place of personal conviction rather than routine.“I got baptized when I was little, but I was feeling a tug on my heart to do it on my terms as an adult! Such a fun, amazing day. Thank you, Jesus,” she wrote.That sense of ownership carried into her conversations with reporters. Faith, she said, has become her anchor through the highs and lows of a WNBA season. “I’ve always been a faithful person, but I just feel like now it’s my own decision.One of the better things I’ve done in my life is getting baptized,” Cunningham said.Later, the Fever star shared, “I think sports and media in general, we need more honesty. We love clickbait. I know that’s what pays the bills and whatnot.”Cunningham is working her way back from a torn MCL suffered late last season, an injury that cut short a campaign where she averaged 8.6 points and brought steady energy on both ends.The recovery has tested her patience, but not her outlook. Even during rehab, she stayed close to the team, supporting from the bench as Indiana pushed deep into the playoffs alongside Caitlin Clark and Lexie Hull. That run, which ended just shy of the Finals, still lingers."I think when you get that close that it hurts even more, right?" Cunningham said. "Like, if you don't even make it to playoffs, you know, 'One day.' But when you're that close to getting there, it just leaves not a great taste in your mouth.”Training camp, by her account, already feels different. "And so I think we're all motivated," she continued. "We don't even have to motivate each other. I think training camp has already been phenomenal."Her perspective has sharpened through all of it. “I don’t care because this is what I do and I love it, but it‘s not who I am deep down, so my faith really keeps me grounded and it allows me to shine and just be myself,” Cunningham said.
Article Summary: Sophie Cunningham is working her way back from a torn MCL suffered late last season. The injury cut short a campaign where she averaged 8.6 points and brought steady energy on both ends. Cunningham shared a video of her adult baptism.
